{{Short description|none}} {{Infobox Film Festival | name = 2005 Cannes Film Festival | image = CFF2005poster.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 58th Cannes Film Festival featuring an original illustration by ''Frédéric Menant'' and ''Tim Garcia''.{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2005/posters/off.html |title=Posters 2005 |work=festival-cannes.fr |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131214070821/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2005/posters/off.html |archive-date=14 December 2013 |df=dmy}} | host = [[Cécile de France]] | number = 21 (Main Competition) | opening = ''[[Lemming (film)|Lemming]]'' | closing = ''[[Chromophobia (film)|Chromophobia]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[L'Enfant (film)|L'Enfant]]'' | date = {{Start date|2005|05|11|df=y}} – {{End date|2005|05|22|df=y}} | founded = 1946 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2004 Cannes Film Festival|2004]] | next = [[2006 Cannes Film Festival|2006]] }} The '''58th [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 11 May and ran until 22 May 2005.{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/festival/2005/theDaily.html |title=Festival de Cannes: 2005 Cannes Film Festival |work=festival-cannes.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091003171414/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/festival/2005/theDaily.html |archive-date=3 October 2009 |df=dmy}} Serbian filmmaker [[Emir Kusturica]] served as jury president for the main competition. [[Cécile de France]] hosted the opening and closing ceremonies.{{cite news |title=Cécile de France, Mistress of Ceremony |url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/fr/theDailyArticle/43060.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131224113909/http://www.festival-cannes.com/fr/theDailyArticle/43060.html |archive-date=24 December 2013 |work=festival-cannes.com |df=dmy}} Belgian filmmakers [[Dardenne brothers|Jean-Pierre and Luc Dardenne]], the Dardenne brothers, won the [[Palme d'Or]] for the second time with the drama film ''[[L'Enfant (film)|L'Enfant]]''.{{cite web|url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2005.htm |title=58ème Festival International du Film - Cannes |work=cinema-francais.fr |language=fr |access-date=26 June 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://www.cannes-fest.com/cine/index.php?a=05 |title=Cannes 2005 / Palmarés |work=cannes-fest.com |language=fr |access-date=21 June 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://www.filmintelligence.org/d-report-y.php?whchyr=2005 |title=58th Cannes Film Festival report by David Robinson |work=filmintelligence.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131212234953/http://www.filmintelligence.org/d-report-y.php?whchyr=2005 |archive-date=12 December 2013 |df=dmy}} The festival opening film was ''[[Lemming (film)|Lemming]]'' directed by [[Dominik Moll]],{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/uk/2005/may/12/cannes2005.cannesfilmfestival |title=Cannes 2005 opening night |work=theguardian.com |access-date=25 May 2017}} while ''[[Chromophobia (film)|Chromophobia]]'' directed by [[Martha Fiennes]] was the closing film.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/uk/2005/apr/20/cannes2005.film |title=British director's film to end Cannes festival |work=theguardian.com |access-date=25 May 2017}} [[File:Un certain regard 05.jpg|thumb|273x273px|2005 [[Un Certain Regard]] poster featuring [[James Dean]]'s portrait by ''Floyd McCarty'' from [[Rebel Without a Cause]].{{cite news|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2005/posters/ucr.html |title=Posters 2005 |work=festival-cannes.fr |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131214070800/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2005/posters/ucr.html |archive-date=14 December 2013
